#pragma once

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics {

struct PageVisit {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> visit_id;
  std::string url;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> visit_time;
};

struct DailyTraffic {
  std::string visit_date;
  int total_visits;
  int unique_pages;
};

struct DailyTrafficView {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "DailyTrafficView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string visit_date;
  int total_visits;
  int unique_pages;
};

std::vector<DailyTraffic> query_daily_traffic_metrics() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Build the query
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<PageVisit>(
      sqlgen::strftime("%Y-%m-%d", &PageVisit::visit_time).as<"visit_date">(),
      sqlgen::count().as<"total_visits">(),
      sqlgen::count_distinct(&PageVisit::url).as<"unique_pages">()
  )
  .group_by(sqlgen::strftime("%Y-%m-%d", &PageVisit::visit_time))
  .order_by(sqlgen::strftime("%Y-%m-%d", &PageVisit::visit_time));

  // Execute the query and convert the results
  return query(conn).and_then([](auto range) {

   #pragma once

   #include <sqlgen.hpp>
   #include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
   #include <vector>
   #include <string>
   #include <optional>

   namespace analyze_temporal_data {

   struct Event {
     sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
     std::string event_name;
     sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
     std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
     sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
   };

   struct TemporalAnalysis {
     int event_id;
     std::string event_name;
     int start_day;
     int start_month;
     int start_year;
     int start_hour;
     int start_minute;
     int start_second;
     int start_weekday;
     int64_t start_epoch;
     std::optional<double> duration_days;
   };

   std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data (const std::vector<Event>& events) {
        // Connect to the database
        auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
        if (!conn_result) {
            return {};
        }
        auto conn = conn_result.value();

        // Create table
        auto create_table_stmt = sqlgen::create_table<Event>;
        auto create_result = create_table_stmt(conn);
        if (!create_result) {
            return {};
        }

        // Insert events
        auto insert_stmt = sqlgen::insert(events);
        auto insert_result = insert_stmt(conn);
        if (!insert_result) {
            return {};
        }

        // Build the query
        using namespace sqlgen::literals;

        auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Event>(
            "event_id"_c,
            "event_name"_c,
            sqlgen::day("start_time"_c).as("start_day"),
            sqlgen::month("start_time"_c).as("start_month"),
            sqlgen::year("start_time"_c).as("start_year"),
            sqlgen::hour("start_time"_c).as("start_hour"),
            sqlgen::minute("start_time"_c).as("start_minute"),
            sqlgen::second("start_time"_c).as("start_second"),
            sqlgen::weekday("start_time"_c).as("start_weekday"),
            sqlgen::unixepoch("start_time"_c).as("start_epoch"),
            sqlgen::days_between("start_time"_c, "end_time"_c).as("duration_days")
        ) | sqlgen::where("end_time"_c.is_not_null());

        // Execute the query
        auto results = query(conn);
        if (!results) {
            return {};
        }

        // Convert results to vector<TemporalAnalysis>
        std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analysis;
        for (const auto& row : *results) {
            TemporalAnalysis ta;
            ta.event_id = std::get<0>(row);
            ta.event_name = std::get<1>(row);
            ta.start_day = std::get<2>(row);
            ta.start_month = std::get<3>(row);
            ta.start_year = std::get<4>(row);
            ta.start_hour = std::get<5>(row);
            ta.start_minute = std::get<6>(row);
            ta.start_second = std::get<7>(row);
            ta.start_weekday = std::get<8>(row);
            ta.start_epoch = std::get<9>(row);
            ta.duration_days = std::get<10>(row);

            analysis.push_back(ta);
        }

        return analysis;
   }

   }  // namespace analyze_temporal_data
